testcase.name: chaos: concurrent lock contention on state files > 10 sequential stateUpdate increments produce correct final count
chaos: concurrent lock contention on state files > 5 concurrent stateUpdate calls serialize correctly
chaos: concurrent lock contention on state files > interleaved reads during writes see consistent snapshots
chaos: concurrent lock contention on state files > stateUpdate after manual file creation works
chaos: SessionResource ref-counting under contention > 100 concurrent acquire/release cycles do not leak resources
chaos: SessionResource ref-counting under contention > acquire during disposal waits for cleanup
chaos: SessionResource ref-counting under contention > double release is safe (no negative refCount)
chaos: corrupt JSON-RPC frames > server ignores corrupt incoming frames without crashing
chaos: corrupt JSON-RPC frames > server handles partial JSON frame (truncated mid-object)
chaos: corrupt JSON-RPC frames > server returns error for frame with missing method field
chaos: corrupt JSON-RPC frames > 100% corruption rate means no valid responses, but server stays alive
chaos: corrupt frame helpers > corruptJsonRpcFrame produces deterministic corruption
chaos: binary frame injection > server survives receiving binary frames instead of text
chaos: server survives idle periods > server responds after 5s idle gap
chaos: server survives idle periods > multiple rapid calls after idle all succeed
chaos: state file corruption recovery > stateRead throws on corrupt JSON (not silently returns default)
chaos: state file corruption recovery > stateUpdate fails on corrupt file, recovers after manual fix
chaos: state file corruption recovery > stateWriteAtomic does not leave .tmp files
chaos: state file corruption recovery > truncated JSON file is detected on read
chaos: state file corruption recovery > sequential stateUpdate calls produce correct final state
chaos: JSONL append under adverse conditions > readJsonl skips malformed lines without crashing
chaos: JSONL append under adverse conditions > appendJsonl respects maxLines cap under rapid writes
chaos: JSONL append under adverse conditions > appendJsonl on non-existent directory creates it
chaos: JSONL append under adverse conditions > readJsonl on non-existent file returns empty without throwing
chaos: job state survives server crash > job file persists across simulated process restart
chaos: watchdog fires correctly under fault conditions > watchdog kills a handler that hangs due to unresponsive WS
chaos: watchdog fires correctly under fault conditions > watchdog does not interfere with a fast handler under latency
chaos: watchdog fires correctly under fault conditions > concurrent watchdogs are independent (no shared timer leaks)
chaos: watchdog fires correctly under fault conditions > external abort races with watchdog - first wins
chaos: watchdog fires correctly under fault conditions > progress callback keeps firing even when handler is slow
chaos: watchdog fires correctly under fault conditions > env override ceiling is respected under fault
chaos: watchdog fires correctly under fault conditions > 10 concurrent calls with different watchdog ceilings resolve independently
chaos: WebSocket disconnect mid-call > client receives error when server kills connection mid-RPC
chaos: WebSocket disconnect mid-call > client reconnects and succeeds after server recovers from disconnect
chaos: WebSocket disconnect mid-call > handles 50% frame drop rate with retries
chaos: WebSocket disconnect mid-call > server terminate (no close frame) triggers error on client
chaos: WebSocket latency injection > calls succeed under high latency without data corruption
